mechanism_narrative: A higher 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS raises equity discount rates and tightens financial conditions, causing lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is the recent change in FEDFUNDS, with larger increases predicting more negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 4.83 as of the latest monthly snapshot available before 2023-05-15
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month FEDFUNDS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated while FEDFUNDS is already above a restrictive threshold, markets price additional policy restraint and lower equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized percent change crosses above 4% while FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 302.918 and FEDFUNDS was 4.83 in the latest snapshot before 2023-05-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not have lower average 5-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations at p < 0.10, or if the triggered hit rate for negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration and rising recession risk, causing investors to lower expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 3.4 in the latest monthly snapshot available before 2023-05-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations, or if triggered average returns exceed unconditional average returns.

mechanism_narrative: Strong PAYEMS growth combined with a low UNRATE indicates durable household income and low layoff risk, supporting consumption and earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 155673 and UNRATE was 3.4 in the latest snapshot before 2023-05-15
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE trigger is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of observations, or if its mean return is not above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth falls below CPIAUCSL inflation growth, real retail demand is contracting, which weakens revenue expectations for consumer-facing firms and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month percent change crosses below CPIAUCSL 3-month percent change
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS was 683042 and CPIAUCSL was 302.918 in the latest snapshot before 2023-05-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered real retail contraction episodes have a nonnegative average 20-day S&P 500 return, or if negative-return hit rate is below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A material decline in HOUST signals weakening rate-sensitive demand and future construction activity, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 6-month moving average by more than 10%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 1420.0 in the latest available snapshot before 2023-05-15
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside threshold episodes are followed by higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered episodes, or if the rolling 3-year directional accuracy is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in INDPRO signals improving production volumes and operating leverage, raising near-term earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 103.0258 in the latest available snapshot before 2023-05-15
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month INDPRO growth is not associated with higher subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: In a restrictive-rate environment, above-trend GDPC1 growth keeps inflation and policy-tightening risk elevated, so good demand news raises expected discount rates more than earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 was 20235.878 and FEDFUNDS was 4.83 in the latest snapshot before 2023-05-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-GDPC1-growth and high-FEDFUNDS trigger produces positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if triggered mean returns exceed unconditional returns.
